summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on zoning case ZC 2023-007 regarding the final development plan for Phase 1 of Woodhall Crossing subdivision. The developer was not present and had requested a continuance because several stipulations from the preliminary approval in December 2023 were still unmet, including tree preservation evidence, building elevations/materials, and complete landscape plans. Residents from both the proposed development area and the adjacent Barkley Woods subdivision voiced concerns about traffic safety on State Route 48, tree clearing, dust, HOA mismanagement by developer Robert, and code enforcement issues. The commission voted to continue the hearing to November 12, 2024.
decisions: Approved minutes from previous hearing on June 4, 2024
Approved continuance of zoning case ZC 2023-007 public hearing to November 12, 2024 at 6:30 PM
action_items: Developer must provide evidence of trees to be preserved on the Woodhall Crossing property
Developer must provide building information including elevations and construction materials for final development plan
Developer must complete landscape plan details for screening and entrance
Developer must submit HOA documents for Woodhall Crossing
Developer must provide a development timeline for each phase of the project
Staff to provide traffic impact study to residents upon request
Staff to clarify whether traffic signal requirement should be tied to a timeline rather than just 132-home threshold
Max Merritt to send materials for two major modifications (Lakefield Place and Health Source) to board members for November meeting
Staff to confirm number of phases in Barkley Woods/Colville Barkley development and provide info to resident via email
key_amounts: $250 per home paid at closing for Barkley Woods HOA emergency reservation fund (23 homes)
$106 credit mentioned by one Barkley Woods resident
$20 fee required for improvement approvals (deck, fence) in Barkley Woods
132 homes is the threshold triggering traffic signal requirement at SR 28 and SR 48
250-foot minimum left turn lane required on State Route 48
notable_topics: Woodhall Crossing subdivision final development plan review and continuance
Traffic impact study approval from ODOT and traffic signal requirements at SR 28 and SR 48
Site distance concerns and hill cutting evaluation on State Route 48
Tree clearing and dust issues on the development property
Screening and mounding requirements for adjacent M1/M2 commercial properties
Barkley Woods HOA financial transparency and mismanagement complaints against developer Robert
Code enforcement issues in Barkley Woods including business operations from homes, parking violations, and HOA non-compliance
Road paving and dedication timeline for new subdivisions
Upcoming major modifications for Lakefield Place and Health Source in November
attendees: Unnamed resident (6468 Snyder Road, Phoenix Industries)
Unnamed resident (368 Berkdale Circle, Barkley Woods)
notes: The developer (Robert, associated with Coach Development) was not present at the hearing despite it being a public hearing on his project. Multiple residents from the adjacent Barkley Woods subdivision expressed serious concerns about the developer's accountability, financial transparency regarding HOA funds, and lack of communication. A resident from Berkdale Circle reported that the developer transferred HOA management without providing financial ledgers. A commercial property owner on Snyder Road expressed concerns about mounding requirements and mentioned the developer had incorrectly accused his property of being an oil contamination source. The commission noted the developer is clearing trees and performing site work before receiving final development plan approval, though this is within their rights on private property. The chairman raised a significant concern about the traffic signal requirement being tied to 132 homes rather than a timeline, citing a past example where Villages of Belmont developer failed to complete road improvements and the township ended up paying.
title: ZC 10-01-2024
